scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, verified against the live machine-readable service catalog (GET https://sonarcloud.io/api/webservices/list, fetched 2026-07-03: 33 web services, 156 cataloged actions) and per-action response examples (GET /api/webservices/response_example?controller=...&action=...). Every non-internal cataloged action across all 33 services is enumerated below, PLUS one live-verified action the catalog itself omits: GET /api/measures/search (this bundle's pre-existing legacy-parity 'measures' stream's real target) is absent from api/webservices/list's api/measures action set (component/component_tree/search_history only) but is confirmed live and functioning (returns 400 with a real validation error for an underspecified query, 200 {"measures":[]} for a valid one; a genuinely unknown action returns 404) -- kept covered_by the measures stream as a documented catalog-vs-reality discrepancy, not dropped. 11 read streams (4 legacy-parity: issues/components/quality_gates/measures, plus 7 newly added: projects/hotspots/languages/metrics/rules/webhooks/project_analyses) and 8 write actions (webhook create/update/delete, issue comment/assign/tags/transition, project tag set) are covered_by a stream or write. Excluded categories used: destructive_admin (permission/user/group/org-membership management -- irreversible or security-sensitive account-scoped mutations far outside a data-sync connector's remit), requires_elevated_scope (actions needing SonarCloud-instance-administrator rights not obtainable via a plain user_token, or actions whose only practical use requires an id sourced from an admin-only prior call), binary_payload (raw source-file byte/text streaming), deprecated (SonarCloud's own webservices catalog marks a deprecatedSince/removal date), non_data_endpoint (auth/session/metadata/introspection actions with no syncable record shape), duplicate_of (a by-id/single-record detail endpoint whose data is already covered by a list stream, or a superseded predecessor of a webservice this catalog explicitly says to use instead), out_of_scope (documented, practical GET/POST endpoints intentionally left out this pass on a breadth-vs-cost basis -- e.g. per-analysis-scoped rule/quality-profile sub-resources, or actions whose only meaningful input is an id this bundle has no stream to source from without a fan-out dependency chain not worth the complexity for a niche resource).

excluded.reason: ends the caller's own HTTP session; not a syncable record and actively counter-productive for a long-lived connector credential
boolean credential-validity probe (true for anonymous too); not a syncable record, and covered functionally by base.check
Compute Engine background-task activity log; a niche operational-monitoring resource, not core SonarCloud analysis data; breadth-vs-cost triage
single-scalar pending/in-progress/failing task-count snapshot, not a syncable record list
per-component Compute Engine task history; a narrower view of the same task data api/ce/activity already exposes, itself out of scope
single-task-by-id detail; a narrower view of the same task data api/ce/activity already exposes, itself out of scope
single-component-by-key detail; identical record shape already reachable via the components list stream's full result set
hierarchical descendant listing under a base component; a differently-shaped traversal of the same component records the components stream already returns flat
per-file cross-project code-duplication block detail, keyed by a specific source file; a deep drill-down resource, not core analysis data; breadth-vs-cost triage
per-user UI favorite-star bookmarking of a project; a personal UI preference, not organizational analysis data or a syncable record
per-user UI favorite-star bookmarking of a project; a personal UI preference, not organizational analysis data or a syncable record
the authenticated user's own personal favorite-project list; a personal UI preference, not organization-scoped analysis data
removed since SonarQube/SonarCloud 6.3; the catalog's own description says to use api/favorites instead (itself excluded above)

Security Hotspots are deprecated and being replaced by security issues/vulnerabilities per the api/hotspots service's own catalog description (deprecatedSince 16 June 2026); the read-side api/hotspots/search is still covered for continuity, but this mutation is not implemented against a deprecated model
single-hotspot-by-key detail; identical record shape already covered by the hotspots list stream's full result set
SCM-account-name autocomplete/typeahead search, not a syncable object list; scoped for UI search-box population

multi-issue batch mutation (assign/transition/tag/comment across an issue-key list in one call); the single-issue mutations (assign_issue/set_issue_tags/do_issue_transition/add_issue_comment) already cover the same primitive operations this dialect's per-record write shape supports, applied one issue at a time
per-issue field-change audit trail; a drill-down detail view, not core issue data already covered by the issues stream
deletes a specific issue comment by its own comment key; a rarely-needed cleanup action with no corresponding read stream of comment keys to source an id from
edits a specific issue comment by its own comment key; a rarely-needed cleanup action with no corresponding read stream of comment keys to source an id from
deprecated 25 Aug 2023 per the catalog; manual issue severity override has been superseded by the impacts/cleanCodeAttribute model
deprecated 25 Aug 2023 per the catalog; manual issue type override has been superseded by the impacts/cleanCodeAttribute model
tag-name autocomplete/typeahead search, not a syncable object list; scoped for UI search-box population
single-component current-measures snapshot; the measures stream's api/measures/search already returns the same metric values across components
hierarchical descendant measures listing; a differently-shaped traversal of the same measure data the measures stream already returns flat

per-component measure time series (one metric's value over successive analyses); a drill-down history view requiring a specific component+metric pairing, not a flat syncable list; breadth-vs-cost triage
deprecated since 7.7 per the catalog
static enum of possible metric value types (INT, PERCENT, ...), not a syncable object list
per-user personal email-notification subscription preference, not organizational analysis data
the authenticated user's own personal notification-subscription list, not organization-scoped analysis data
per-user personal email-notification subscription preference, not organizational analysis data
grants a permission to a group at global or project scope; account/access-control administration, out of remit for a data-sync connector
permission-template administration; account/access-control administration, out of remit
permission-template administration; account/access-control administration, out of remit
grants a permission to a user at global or project scope; account/access-control administration, out of remit
permission-template administration; account/access-control administration, out of remit
applies a permission template to a project, overwriting its existing access grants; account/access-control administration, out of remit

irreversibly deletes a permission template; account/access-control administration, out of remit
revokes a permission from a group; account/access-control administration, out of remit

permission-template administration; account/access-control administration, out of remit
revokes a permission from a user; account/access-control administration, out of remit
permission-template administration; account/access-control administration, out of remit
lists permission templates; requires global administrator rights and only serves the excluded permission-administration write actions above
changes which permission template new projects get by default; account/access-control administration, out of remit
permission-template administration; account/access-control administration, out of remit

annotates a specific analysis with a custom marker event; a niche write with no corresponding common sync use case, and its target analysis key is only obtainable via the already-covered project_analyses read stream, adding a fan-out dependency not worth the complexity for this action alone
irreversibly deletes a historical analysis snapshot and all its associated measures/issues-at-that-point-in-time; destructive project-data administration, out of remit
irreversibly deletes an analysis marker event; destructive project-data administration
changes which historical analysis the New Code Definition is measured against; project-administrator-scoped configuration, not a data-sync mutation
reverts the New Code Definition baseline; project-administrator-scoped configuration, not a data-sync mutation

renames a specific analysis marker event; a niche write with no corresponding common sync use case, and its target event key is only obtainable via the already-covered project_analyses read stream's nested events[], adding a fan-out dependency not worth the complexity for this action alone
returns an SVG image badge, not JSON record data
returns an SVG image badge, not JSON record data
returns an SVG image badge, not JSON record data
irreversibly deletes a project branch and its analysis history; destructive project-data administration, out of remit

per-project branch inventory (name/status/analysis-date); a scoping/navigation resource rather than core analysis data, and this bundle has no per-project fan-out mechanism configured to enumerate every project's branches without adding one; breadth-vs-cost triage
renames a project's main branch; project-administrator-scoped configuration, not a data-sync mutation
manages a project's auxiliary reference links (homepage/CI/issue-tracker URLs); a project-metadata convenience field, not core analysis data
manages a project's auxiliary reference links; a project-metadata convenience field, not core analysis data
lists a project's auxiliary reference links; a project-metadata convenience field, not core analysis data; breadth-vs-cost triage
irreversibly deletes a project pull-request analysis and its history; destructive project-data administration, out of remit

per-project open-pull-request analysis inventory; a scoping/navigation resource rather than core analysis data, and this bundle has no per-project fan-out mechanism configured to enumerate every project's pull requests without adding one; breadth-vs-cost triage

returns a bare JSON array of tag-name strings (not objects); connsdk.RecordsAt only explodes array elements that decode as a JSON object, so a scalar-string array yields zero records under this dialect -- there is no declarative way to wrap each string into a syncable record without a computed_fields/schema mechanism this dialect does not have for bare-array responses
irreversibly deletes multiple projects and all their analysis history in one call; destructive project-data administration, out of remit
deprecated since 7.6 per the catalog
requires the 'Create Projects' organization permission per the action's own description; project provisioning is an organization-administration action, not a typical data-sync mutation
irreversibly deletes a project and all its analysis history; destructive project-data administration, out of remit

renames a project's unique key, which is also its primary-key identifier for every other stream in this bundle; requires project-administrator rights and changing it would break cross-stream record correlation
changes a project's public/private visibility; requires project-administrator rights, an access-control change rather than a data-sync mutation
